proposed_relations.description: MTA New York City Transit and NYC DOT jointly presented the Nostrand & Rogers Select Bus Service Community Advisory Committee slides on December 8, 2009.

evidence_refs.source_quote: A photograph of a city street scene in Brooklyn, featuring a bus stop with a blue tactile paving strip, a bus, and pedestrians. The image includes logos for MTA New York City Transit and New York City DOT, and text identifying the Nostrand & Rogers Select Bus Service Community Advisory Committee meeting at Brooklyn College on December 8, 2009.

rationale: Observation already resolves to base_record_id entity_mta-nyct. Block p001_c0002 shows the MTA New York City Transit logo, confirming this is the same agency — MTA New York City Transit, entity_type agency. The observation's own base_record_id is the closest match.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The logo for the Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA) New York City Transit, featuring the MTA globe icon and the text "MTA New York City Transit".

rationale: Observation already resolves to base_record_id entity_nyc-dot. Block p001_c0003 shows the NYC DOT logo, confirming this is the same agency — New York City Department of Transportation, entity_type agency. The observation's own base_record_id is the closest match.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The logo for the New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT), featuring the text "NEW YORK CITY" above a stylized "DOT" graphic.

rationale: Observation's base_record_id 'project_nostrand-rogers-sbs' already exists in the registry and is the correct canonical record for this project. The registry record includes this same source (20091208_nostrand_cac_slides) in its source_ids, and both the observation payload and registry record describe the same real-world project: proposed SBS on Nostrand Avenue (southbound) and Rogers Avenue (northbound), converting B44 Limited to SBS in Brooklyn. Per canonicalization rules, when the closest match is the observation's own base_record_id, the decision is 'new' — the observation already resolves to that id.
